**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
 (`sqlalchemy.util.langhelpers.MemoizedSlots`)

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
session[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.session "Link to this definition")

The [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 in use.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
statement[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.statement "Link to this definition")

The SQL statement being invoked. For an ORM selection as would be retrieved from [`Query`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/query.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Query "sqlalchemy.orm.Query")
, this is an instance of [`select`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/future.html#sqlalchemy.future.select "sqlalchemy.future.select")
 that was generated from the ORM query.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
parameters[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.parameters "Link to this definition")

Dictionary of parameters that was passed to [`Session.execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
bind\_arguments[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_arguments "Link to this definition")

The dictionary passed as the [`Session.execute.bind_arguments`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute.params.bind_arguments "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
 dictionary. This dictionary may be used by extensions to [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 to pass arguments that will assist in determining amongst a set of database connections which one should be used to invoke this statement.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
local\_execution\_options[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.local_execution_options "Link to this definition")

Dictionary view of the execution options passed to the [`Session.execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
 method. This does not include options that may be associated with the statement being invoked.

See also

[`ORMExecuteState.execution_options`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.execution_options "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.execution_options")

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
execution\_options[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.execution_options "Link to this definition")

The complete dictionary of current execution options. This is a merge of the statement level options with the locally passed execution options.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
all\_mappers[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.all_mappers "Link to this definition")

Return a sequence of all [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
 objects that are involved at the top level of this statement.

By “top level” we mean those [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
 objects that would be represented in the result set rows for a [`select()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.select "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.select")
 query, or for a [`update()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.update "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.update")
 or [`delete()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.delete "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.delete")
 query, the mapper that is the main subject of the UPDATE or DELETE.

Added in version 1.4.0b2.

See also

[`ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper")

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
bind\_mapper[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper "Link to this definition")

Return the [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
 that is the primary “bind” mapper.

For an [`ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
 object invoking an ORM statement, that is, the [`ORMExecuteState.is_orm_statement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_orm_statement "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_orm_statement")
 attribute is `True`, this attribute will return the [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
 that is considered to be the “primary” mapper of the statement. The term “bind mapper” refers to the fact that a [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 object may be “bound” to multiple [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
 objects keyed to mapped classes, and the “bind mapper” determines which of those [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
 objects would be selected.

For a statement that is invoked against a single mapped class, [`ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_mapper")
 is intended to be a reliable way of getting this mapper.

Added in version 1.4.0b2.

See also

[`ORMExecuteState.all_mappers`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.all_mappers "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.all_mappers")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
invoke\_statement(_statement\=None_, _params\=None_, _execution\_options\=None_, _bind\_arguments\=None_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement "Link to this definition")

Execute the statement represented by this [`ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
, without re-invoking events that have already proceeded.

This method essentially performs a re-entrant execution of the current statement for which the [`SessionEvents.do_orm_execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/events.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionEvents.do_orm_execute "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionEvents.do_orm_execute")
 event is being currently invoked. The use case for this is for event handlers that want to override how the ultimate [`Result`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Result "sqlalchemy.engine.Result")
 object is returned, such as for schemes that retrieve results from an offline cache or which concatenate results from multiple executions.

When the [`Result`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Result "sqlalchemy.engine.Result")
 object is returned by the actual handler function within [`SessionEvents.do_orm_execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/events.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionEvents.do_orm_execute "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionEvents.do_orm_execute")
 and is propagated to the calling [`Session.execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
 method, the remainder of the [`Session.execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
 method is preempted and the [`Result`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Result "sqlalchemy.engine.Result")
 object is returned to the caller of [`Session.execute()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.execute")
 immediately.

Parameters:

*   **statement**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement.params.statement)
     – optional statement to be invoked, in place of the statement currently represented by [`ORMExecuteState.statement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.statement "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.statement")
    .
    
*   **params**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement.params.params)
     – optional dictionary of parameters which will be merged into the existing [`ORMExecuteState.parameters`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.parameters "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.parameters")
     of this [`ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
    .
    
*   **execution\_options**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement.params.execution_options)
     – optional dictionary of execution options will be merged into the existing [`ORMExecuteState.execution_options`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.execution_options "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.execution_options")
     of this [`ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
    .
    
*   **bind\_arguments**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement.params.bind_arguments)
     – optional dictionary of bind\_arguments which will be merged amongst the current [`ORMExecuteState.bind_arguments`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_arguments "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.bind_arguments")
     of this [`ORMExecuteState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState")
    .
    

Returns:

a [`Result`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Result "sqlalchemy.engine.Result")
 object with ORM-level results.

See also

[Re-Executing Statements](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_events.html#do-orm-execute-re-executing)
 - background and examples on the appropriate usage of [`ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.invoke_statement")
.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_column\_load[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_column_load "Link to this definition")

Return True if the operation is refreshing column-oriented attributes on an existing ORM object.

This occurs during operations such as [`Session.refresh()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.refresh "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.refresh")
, as well as when an attribute deferred by [`defer()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/loading_columns.html#sqlalchemy.orm.defer "sqlalchemy.orm.defer")
 is being loaded, or an attribute that was expired either directly by [`Session.expire()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.expire "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.expire")
 or via a commit operation is being loaded.

Handlers will very likely not want to add any options to queries when such an operation is occurring as the query should be a straight primary key fetch which should not have any additional WHERE criteria, and loader options travelling with the instance will have already been added to the query.

Added in version 1.4.0b2.

See also

[`ORMExecuteState.is_relationship_load`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_relationship_load "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_relationship_load")

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_delete[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_delete "Link to this definition")

return True if this is a DELETE operation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_insert[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_insert "Link to this definition")

return True if this is an INSERT operation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_orm\_statement[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_orm_statement "Link to this definition")

return True if the operation is an ORM statement.

This indicates that the select(), update(), or delete() being invoked contains ORM entities as subjects. For a statement that does not have ORM entities and instead refers only to [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
 metadata, it is invoked as a Core SQL statement and no ORM-level automation takes place.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_relationship\_load[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_relationship_load "Link to this definition")

Return True if this load is loading objects on behalf of a relationship.

This means, the loader in effect is either a LazyLoader, SelectInLoader, SubqueryLoader, or similar, and the entire SELECT statement being emitted is on behalf of a relationship load.

Handlers will very likely not want to add any options to queries when such an operation is occurring, as loader options are already capable of being propagated to relationship loaders and should be already present.

See also

[`ORMExecuteState.is_column_load`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_column_load "sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_column_load")

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_select[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_select "Link to this definition")

return True if this is a SELECT operation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
is\_update[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.is_update "Link to this definition")

return True if this is an UPDATE operation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
lazy\_loaded\_from[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.lazy_loaded_from "Link to this definition")

An [`InstanceState`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/internals.html#sqlalchemy.orm.InstanceState "sqlalchemy.orm.InstanceState")
 that is using this statement execution for a lazy load operation.

The primary rationale for this attribute is to support the horizontal sharding extension, where it is available within specific query execution time hooks created by this extension. To that end, the attribute is only intended to be meaningful at **query execution time**, and importantly not any time prior to that, including query compilation time.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
load\_options[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.load_options "Link to this definition")

Return the load\_options that will be used for this execution.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
loader\_strategy\_path[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.loader_strategy_path "Link to this definition")

Return the `PathRegistry` for the current load path.

This object represents the “path” in a query along relationships when a particular object or collection is being loaded.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
update\_delete\_options[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.update_delete_options "Link to this definition")

Return the update\_delete\_options that will be used for this execution.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState)
user\_defined\_options[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.ORMExecuteState.user_defined_options "Link to this definition")

The sequence of `UserDefinedOptions` that have been associated with the statement being invoked.

_class_ sqlalchemy.orm.Session(_bind\=None_, _autoflush\=True_, _future\=False_, _expire\_on\_commit\=True_, _autocommit\=False_, _twophase\=False_, _binds\=None_, _enable\_baked\_queries\=True_, _info\=None_, _query\_cls\=None_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "Link to this definition")

Manages persistence operations for ORM-mapped objects.

The Session’s usage paradigm is described at [Using the Session](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session.html)
.

, [get\_bind()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind)
, [get\_nested\_transaction()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_nested_transaction)
, [get\_transaction()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_transaction)

, [identity\_map](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.identity_map)
, [in\_nested\_transaction()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.in_nested_transaction)
, [in\_transaction()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.in_transaction)

, [invalidate()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.invalidate)
, [is\_active](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.is_active)
, [is\_modified()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.is_modified)

**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 (`sqlalchemy.orm.session._SessionClassMethods`)

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
\_\_init\_\_(_bind\=None_, _autoflush\=True_, _future\=False_, _expire\_on\_commit\=True_, _autocommit\=False_, _twophase\=False_, _binds\=None_, _enable\_baked\_queries\=True_, _info\=None_, _query\_cls\=None_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Construct a new Session.

See also the [`sessionmaker`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker "sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker")
 function which is used to generate a [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
\-producing callable with a given set of arguments.

Parameters:

*   **autocommit**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.autocommit)
     – Defaults to `False`. When `True`, the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
     does not automatically begin transactions for individual statement executions, will acquire connections from the engine on an as-needed basis, releasing to the connection pool after each statement. Flushes will begin and commit (or possibly rollback) their own transaction if no transaction is present. When using this mode, the [`Session.begin()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin")
     method may be used to explicitly start transactions, but the usual “autobegin” behavior is not present.
    
*   **autoflush**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.autoflush)
     –
    
    When `True`, all query operations will issue a [`Session.flush()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush")
     call to this `Session` before proceeding. This is a convenience feature so that [`Session.flush()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush")
     need not be called repeatedly in order for database queries to retrieve results. It’s typical that `autoflush` is used in conjunction with `autocommit=False`. In this scenario, explicit calls to [`Session.flush()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush")
     are rarely needed; you usually only need to call [`Session.commit()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit")
     (which flushes) to finalize changes.
    
    See also
    
    [Flushing](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-flushing)
     - additional background on autoflush
    
*   **bind**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.bind)
     – An optional [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     to which this `Session` should be bound. When specified, all SQL operations performed by this session will execute via this connectable.
    
*   **binds**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.binds)
     –
    
    A dictionary which may specify any number of [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     objects as the source of connectivity for SQL operations on a per-entity basis. The keys of the dictionary consist of any series of mapped classes, arbitrary Python classes that are bases for mapped classes, [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
     objects and [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
     objects. The values of the dictionary are then instances of [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     or less commonly [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     objects. Operations which proceed relative to a particular mapped class will consult this dictionary for the closest matching entity in order to determine which [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     should be used for a particular SQL operation. The complete heuristics for resolution are described at [`Session.get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
    . Usage looks like:
    
    Session \= sessionmaker(binds\={
        SomeMappedClass: create\_engine('postgresql://engine1'),
        SomeDeclarativeBase: create\_engine('postgresql://engine2'),
        some\_mapper: create\_engine('postgresql://engine3'),
        some\_table: create\_engine('postgresql://engine4'),
        })
    
    See also
    
    [Partitioning Strategies (e.g. multiple database backends per Session)](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#session-partitioning)
    
    [`Session.bind_mapper()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper")
    
    [`Session.bind_table()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table")
    
    [`Session.get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
    
*   **class\_**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.class_)
     – Specify an alternate class other than `sqlalchemy.orm.session.Session` which should be used by the returned class. This is the only argument that is local to the [`sessionmaker`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker "sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker")
     function, and is not sent directly to the constructor for `Session`.
    
*   **enable\_baked\_queries**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.enable_baked_queries)
     –
    
    defaults to `True`. A flag consumed by the [`sqlalchemy.ext.baked`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/extensions/baked.html#module-sqlalchemy.ext.baked "sqlalchemy.ext.baked")
     extension to determine if “baked queries” should be cached, as is the normal operation of this extension. When set to `False`, caching as used by this particular extension is disabled.
    
    Changed in version 1.4: The `sqlalchemy.ext.baked` extension is legacy and is not used by any of SQLAlchemy’s internals. This flag therefore only affects applications that are making explicit use of this extension within their own code.
    
*   **expire\_on\_commit**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.expire_on_commit)
     –
    
    Defaults to `True`. When `True`, all instances will be fully expired after each [`commit()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit")
    , so that all attribute/object access subsequent to a completed transaction will load from the most recent database state.
    
    > See also
    > 
    > [Committing](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-committing)
    
*   **future**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.future)
     –
    
    if True, use 2.0 style transactional and engine behavior. Future mode includes the following behaviors:
    
    *   The [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
         will not use “bound” metadata in order to locate an [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
        ; the engine or engines in use must be specified to the constructor of [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
         or otherwise be configured against the [`sessionmaker`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker "sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker")
         in use
        
    *   The “subtransactions” feature of [`Session.begin()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin")
         is removed in version 2.0 and is disabled when the future flag is set.
        
    *   The behavior of the [`relationship.cascade_backrefs`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/relationship_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.cascade_backrefs "sqlalchemy.orm.relationship")
         flag on a [`relationship()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/relationship_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship "sqlalchemy.orm.relationship")
         will always assume “False” behavior.
        
    
    Added in version 1.4.
    
    See also
    
    [Migrating to SQLAlchemy 2.0](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/changelog/migration_20.html)
    
*   **info**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.info)
     – optional dictionary of arbitrary data to be associated with this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
    . Is available via the [`Session.info`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.info "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.info")
     attribute. Note the dictionary is copied at construction time so that modifications to the per- [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
     dictionary will be local to that [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
    .
    
*   **query\_cls**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.query_cls)
     – Class which should be used to create new Query objects, as returned by the [`Session.query()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.query "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.query")
     method. Defaults to [`Query`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/query.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Query "sqlalchemy.orm.Query")
    .
    
*   **twophase**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.twophase)
     – When `True`, all transactions will be started as a “two phase” transaction, i.e. using the “two phase” semantics of the database in use along with an XID. During a [`commit()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit")
    , after [`flush()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.flush")
     has been issued for all attached databases, the [`TwoPhaseTransaction.prepare()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.TwoPhaseTransaction.prepare "sqlalchemy.engine.TwoPhaseTransaction.prepare")
     method on each database’s [`TwoPhaseTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.TwoPhaseTransaction "sqlalchemy.engine.TwoPhaseTransaction")
     will be called. This allows each database to roll back the entire transaction, before each transaction is committed.
    

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
add(_instance_, _\_warn\=True_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "Link to this definition")

Place an object into this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
.

Objects that are in the [transient](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-transient)
 state when passed to the [`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")
 method will move to the [pending](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-pending)
 state, until the next flush, at which point they will move to the [persistent](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-persistent)
 state.

Objects that are in the [detached](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-detached)
 state when passed to the [`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")
 method will move to the [persistent](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-persistent)
 state directly.

If the transaction used by the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 is rolled back, objects which were transient when they were passed to [`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")
 will be moved back to the [transient](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-transient)
 state, and will no longer be present within this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
.

See also

[`Session.add_all()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all")

[Adding New or Existing Items](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-adding)
 - at [Basics of Using a Session](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#id1)

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
add\_all(_instances_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all "Link to this definition")

Add the given collection of instances to this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
.

See the documentation for [`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")
 for a general behavioral description.

See also

[`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")

[Adding New or Existing Items](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-adding)
 - at [Basics of Using a Session](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#id1)

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
begin(_subtransactions\=False_, _nested\=False_, _\_subtrans\=False_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "Link to this definition")

Begin a transaction, or nested transaction, on this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
, if one is not already begun.

The [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 object features **autobegin** behavior, so that normally it is not necessary to call the [`Session.begin()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin")
 method explicitly. However, it may be used in order to control the scope of when the transactional state is begun.

When used to begin the outermost transaction, an error is raised if this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 is already inside of a transaction.

Parameters:

*   **nested**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin.params.nested)
     – if True, begins a SAVEPOINT transaction and is equivalent to calling [`Session.begin_nested()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested")
    . For documentation on SAVEPOINT transactions, please see [Using SAVEPOINT](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-begin-nested)
    .
    
*   **subtransactions**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin.params.subtransactions)
     – if True, indicates that this [`Session.begin()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin")
     can create a “subtransaction”.
    

Returns:

the [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 object. Note that [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 acts as a Python context manager, allowing [`Session.begin()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin")
 to be used in a “with” block. See [Explicit Begin](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-autocommit)
 for an example.

See also

[Auto Begin](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-autobegin)

[Managing Transactions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#unitofwork-transaction)

[`Session.begin_nested()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
begin\_nested()[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested "Link to this definition")

Begin a “nested” transaction on this Session, e.g. SAVEPOINT.

The target database(s) and associated drivers must support SQL SAVEPOINT for this method to function correctly.

For documentation on SAVEPOINT transactions, please see [Using SAVEPOINT](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-begin-nested)
.

Returns:

the [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 object. Note that [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 acts as a context manager, allowing [`Session.begin_nested()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested")
 to be used in a “with” block. See [Using SAVEPOINT](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-begin-nested)
 for a usage example.

See also

[Using SAVEPOINT](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-begin-nested)

[Serializable isolation / Savepoints / Transactional DDL](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/dialects/sqlite.html#pysqlite-serializable)
 - special workarounds required with the SQLite driver in order for SAVEPOINT to work correctly.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
bind\_mapper(_mapper_, _bind_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper "Link to this definition")

Associate a [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
 or arbitrary Python class with a “bind”, e.g. an [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
 or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
.

The given entity is added to a lookup used by the [`Session.get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
 method.

Parameters:

*   **mapper**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper.params.mapper)
     – a [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
     object, or an instance of a mapped class, or any Python class that is the base of a set of mapped classes.
    
*   **bind**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper.params.bind)
     – an [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     object.
    

See also

[Partitioning Strategies (e.g. multiple database backends per Session)](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#session-partitioning)

[`Session.binds`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.binds "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")

[`Session.bind_table()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
bind\_table(_table_, _bind_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table "Link to this definition")

Associate a [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
 with a “bind”, e.g. an [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
 or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
.

The given [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
 is added to a lookup used by the [`Session.get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
 method.

Parameters:

*   **table**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table.params.table)
     – a [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
     object, which is typically the target of an ORM mapping, or is present within a selectable that is mapped.
    
*   **bind**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_table.params.bind)
     – an [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
     or [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     object.
    

See also

[Partitioning Strategies (e.g. multiple database backends per Session)](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#session-partitioning)

[`Session.binds`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.binds "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")

[`Session.bind_mapper()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bind_mapper")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
bulk\_insert\_mappings(_mapper_, _mappings_, _return\_defaults\=False_, _render\_nulls\=False_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings "Link to this definition")

Perform a bulk insert of the given list of mapping dictionaries.

The bulk insert feature allows plain Python dictionaries to be used as the source of simple INSERT operations which can be more easily grouped together into higher performing “executemany” operations. Using dictionaries, there is no “history” or session state management features in use, reducing latency when inserting large numbers of simple rows.

The values within the dictionaries as given are typically passed without modification into Core [`sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Insert()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Insert "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Insert")
 constructs, after organizing the values within them across the tables to which the given mapper is mapped.

Added in version 1.0.0.

Legacy Feature

The bulk insert feature allows for a lower-latency INSERT of rows at the expense of most other unit-of-work features. Features such as object management, relationship handling, and SQL clause support are silently omitted in favor of raw INSERT of records.

In SQLAlchemy 2.0, improved versions of the bulk insert/update methods are introduced, with clearer behavior and documentation, new capabilities, and much better performance.

For 1.4 use, **please read the list of caveats at** [ORM Compatibility / Caveats](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations-caveats)
 **before using this method, and fully test and confirm the functionality of all code developed using these systems.**

Parameters:

*   **mapper**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings.params.mapper)
     – a mapped class, or the actual [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
     object, representing the single kind of object represented within the mapping list.
    
*   **mappings**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings.params.mappings)
     – a sequence of dictionaries, each one containing the state of the mapped row to be inserted, in terms of the attribute names on the mapped class. If the mapping refers to multiple tables, such as a joined-inheritance mapping, each dictionary must contain all keys to be populated into all tables.
    
*   **return\_defaults**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings.params.return_defaults)
     – when True, rows that are missing values which generate defaults, namely integer primary key defaults and sequences, will be inserted **one at a time**, so that the primary key value is available. In particular this will allow joined-inheritance and other multi-table mappings to insert correctly without the need to provide primary key values ahead of time; however, [`Session.bulk_insert_mappings.return_defaults`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings.params.return_defaults "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings")
     **greatly reduces the performance gains** of the method overall. If the rows to be inserted only refer to a single table, then there is no reason this flag should be set as the returned default information is not used.
    
*   **render\_nulls**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings.params.render_nulls)
     –
    
    When True, a value of `None` will result in a NULL value being included in the INSERT statement, rather than the column being omitted from the INSERT. This allows all the rows being INSERTed to have the identical set of columns which allows the full set of rows to be batched to the DBAPI. Normally, each column-set that contains a different combination of NULL values than the previous row must omit a different series of columns from the rendered INSERT statement, which means it must be emitted as a separate statement. By passing this flag, the full set of rows are guaranteed to be batchable into one batch; the cost however is that server-side defaults which are invoked by an omitted column will be skipped, so care must be taken to ensure that these are not necessary.
    
    Warning
    
    When this flag is set, **server side default SQL values will not be invoked** for those columns that are inserted as NULL; the NULL value will be sent explicitly. Care must be taken to ensure that no server-side default functions need to be invoked for the operation as a whole.
    
    Added in version 1.1.
    

See also

[Bulk Operations](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations)

[`Session.bulk_save_objects()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects")

[`Session.bulk_update_mappings()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
bulk\_save\_objects(_objects_, _return\_defaults\=False_, _update\_changed\_only\=True_, _preserve\_order\=True_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects "Link to this definition")

Perform a bulk save of the given list of objects.

The bulk save feature allows mapped objects to be used as the source of simple INSERT and UPDATE operations which can be more easily grouped together into higher performing “executemany” operations; the extraction of data from the objects is also performed using a lower-latency process that ignores whether or not attributes have actually been modified in the case of UPDATEs, and also ignores SQL expressions.

The objects as given are not added to the session and no additional state is established on them. If the [`Session.bulk_save_objects.return_defaults`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.return_defaults "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects")
 flag is set, then server-generated primary key values will be assigned to the returned objects, but **not server side defaults**; this is a limitation in the implementation. If stateful objects are desired, please use the standard [`Session.add_all()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all")
 approach or as an alternative newer mass-insert features such as [Using INSERT, UPDATE and ON CONFLICT (i.e. upsert) to return ORM Objects](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#orm-dml-returning-objects)
.

Legacy Feature

The bulk save feature allows for a lower-latency INSERT/UPDATE of rows at the expense of most other unit-of-work features. Features such as object management, relationship handling, and SQL clause support are silently omitted in favor of raw INSERT/UPDATES of records.

In SQLAlchemy 2.0, improved versions of the bulk insert/update methods are introduced, with clearer behavior and documentation, new capabilities, and much better performance.

For 1.4 use, **please read the list of caveats at** [ORM Compatibility / Caveats](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations-caveats)
 **before using this method, and fully test and confirm the functionality of all code developed using these systems.**

Parameters:

*   **objects**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.objects)
     –
    
    a sequence of mapped object instances. The mapped objects are persisted as is, and are **not** associated with the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
     afterwards.
    
    For each object, whether the object is sent as an INSERT or an UPDATE is dependent on the same rules used by the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
     in traditional operation; if the object has the `InstanceState.key` attribute set, then the object is assumed to be “detached” and will result in an UPDATE. Otherwise, an INSERT is used.
    
    In the case of an UPDATE, statements are grouped based on which attributes have changed, and are thus to be the subject of each SET clause. If `update_changed_only` is False, then all attributes present within each object are applied to the UPDATE statement, which may help in allowing the statements to be grouped together into a larger executemany(), and will also reduce the overhead of checking history on attributes.
    
*   **return\_defaults**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.return_defaults)
     – when True, rows that are missing values which generate defaults, namely integer primary key defaults and sequences, will be inserted **one at a time**, so that the primary key value is available. In particular this will allow joined-inheritance and other multi-table mappings to insert correctly without the need to provide primary key values ahead of time; however, [`Session.bulk_save_objects.return_defaults`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.return_defaults "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects")
     **greatly reduces the performance gains** of the method overall. It is strongly advised to please use the standard [`Session.add_all()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add_all")
     approach.
    
*   **update\_changed\_only**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.update_changed_only)
     – when True, UPDATE statements are rendered based on those attributes in each state that have logged changes. When False, all attributes present are rendered into the SET clause with the exception of primary key attributes.
    
*   **preserve\_order**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects.params.preserve_order)
     –
    
    when True, the order of inserts and updates matches exactly the order in which the objects are given. When False, common types of objects are grouped into inserts and updates, to allow for more batching opportunities.
    
    Added in version 1.3.
    

See also

[Bulk Operations](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations)

[`Session.bulk_insert_mappings()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings")

[`Session.bulk_update_mappings()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
bulk\_update\_mappings(_mapper_, _mappings_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings "Link to this definition")

Perform a bulk update of the given list of mapping dictionaries.

The bulk update feature allows plain Python dictionaries to be used as the source of simple UPDATE operations which can be more easily grouped together into higher performing “executemany” operations. Using dictionaries, there is no “history” or session state management features in use, reducing latency when updating large numbers of simple rows.

Added in version 1.0.0.

Legacy Feature

The bulk update feature allows for a lower-latency UPDATE of rows at the expense of most other unit-of-work features. Features such as object management, relationship handling, and SQL clause support are silently omitted in favor of raw UPDATES of records.

In SQLAlchemy 2.0, improved versions of the bulk insert/update methods are introduced, with clearer behavior and documentation, new capabilities, and much better performance.

For 1.4 use, **please read the list of caveats at** [ORM Compatibility / Caveats](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations-caveats)
 **before using this method, and fully test and confirm the functionality of all code developed using these systems.**

Parameters:

*   **mapper**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings.params.mapper)
     – a mapped class, or the actual [`Mapper`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/mapping_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper "sqlalchemy.orm.Mapper")
     object, representing the single kind of object represented within the mapping list.
    
*   **mappings**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_update_mappings.params.mappings)
     – a sequence of dictionaries, each one containing the state of the mapped row to be updated, in terms of the attribute names on the mapped class. If the mapping refers to multiple tables, such as a joined-inheritance mapping, each dictionary may contain keys corresponding to all tables. All those keys which are present and are not part of the primary key are applied to the SET clause of the UPDATE statement; the primary key values, which are required, are applied to the WHERE clause.
    

See also

[Bulk Operations](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/persistence_techniques.html#bulk-operations)

[`Session.bulk_insert_mappings()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_insert_mappings")

[`Session.bulk_save_objects()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.bulk_save_objects")

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
close()[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close "Link to this definition")

Close out the transactional resources and ORM objects used by this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
.

This expunges all ORM objects associated with this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
, ends any transaction in progress and [releases](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-releases)
 any [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
 objects which this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 itself has checked out from associated [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
 objects. The operation then leaves the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 in a state which it may be used again.

Tip

The [`Session.close()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close")
 method **does not prevent the Session from being used again**. The [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 itself does not actually have a distinct “closed” state; it merely means the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 will release all database connections and ORM objects.

Changed in version 1.4: The [`Session.close()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close")
 method does not immediately create a new [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 object; instead, the new [`SessionTransaction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction "sqlalchemy.orm.SessionTransaction")
 is created only if the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 is used again for a database operation.

See also

[Closing](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-closing)
 - detail on the semantics of [`Session.close()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close")

_classmethod_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
close\_all()[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close_all "Link to this definition")

_inherited from the_ `sqlalchemy.orm.session._SessionClassMethods.close_all` _method of_ `sqlalchemy.orm.session._SessionClassMethods`

Close _all_ sessions in memory.

Deprecated since version 1.3: The [`Session.close_all()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close_all "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.close_all")
 method is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Please refer to `close_all_sessions()`.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
commit()[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit "Link to this definition")

Flush pending changes and commit the current transaction.

When the COMMIT operation is complete, all objects are fully [expired](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-expired)
, erasing their internal contents, which will be automatically re-loaded when the objects are next accessed. In the interim, these objects are in an expired state and will not function if they are [detached](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-detached)
 from the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
. Additionally, this re-load operation is not supported when using asyncio-oriented APIs. The [`Session.expire_on_commit`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.expire_on_commit "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 parameter may be used to disable this behavior.

When there is no transaction in place for the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
, indicating that no operations were invoked on this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 since the previous call to [`Session.commit()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.commit")
, the method will begin and commit an internal-only “logical” transaction, that does not normally affect the database unless pending flush changes were detected, but will still invoke event handlers and object expiration rules.

If [1.x-style](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-0)
 use is in effect and there are currently SAVEPOINTs in progress via [`Session.begin_nested()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.begin_nested")
, the operation will release the current SAVEPOINT but not commit the outermost database transaction.

If [2.0-style](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-1)
 use is in effect via the [`Session.future`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.params.future "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 flag, the outermost database transaction is committed unconditionally, automatically releasing any SAVEPOINTs in effect.

When using legacy “autocommit” mode, this method is only valid to call if a transaction is actually in progress, else an error is raised. Similarly, when using legacy “subtransactions”, the method will instead close out the current “subtransaction”, rather than the actual database transaction, if a transaction is in progress.

See also

[Committing](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-committing)

[Managing Transactions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#unitofwork-transaction)

[Preventing Implicit IO when Using AsyncSession](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/extensions/asyncio.html#asyncio-orm-avoid-lazyloads)

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
connection(_bind\_arguments\=None_, _close\_with\_result\=False_, _execution\_options\=None_, _\*\*kw_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection "Link to this definition")

Return a [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
 object corresponding to this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 object’s transactional state.

If this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 is configured with `autocommit=False`, either the [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
 corresponding to the current transaction is returned, or if no transaction is in progress, a new one is begun and the [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
 returned (note that no transactional state is established with the DBAPI until the first SQL statement is emitted).

Alternatively, if this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 is configured with `autocommit=True`, an ad-hoc [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
 is returned using [`Engine.connect()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.connect "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.connect")
 on the underlying [`Engine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine")
.

Ambiguity in multi-bind or unbound [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 objects can be resolved through any of the optional keyword arguments. This ultimately makes usage of the [`get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
 method for resolution.

Parameters:

*   **bind\_arguments**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.bind_arguments)
     – dictionary of bind arguments. May include “mapper”, “bind”, “clause”, other custom arguments that are passed to [`Session.get_bind()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.get_bind")
    .
    
*   **bind**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.bind)
     – deprecated; use bind\_arguments
    
*   **mapper**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.mapper)
     – deprecated; use bind\_arguments
    
*   **clause**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.clause)
     – deprecated; use bind\_arguments
    
*   **close\_with\_result**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.close_with_result)
     –
    
    Passed to [`Engine.connect()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.connect "sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.connect")
    , indicating the [`Connection`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection")
     should be considered “single use”, automatically closing when the first result set is closed. This flag only has an effect if this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
     is configured with `autocommit=True` and does not already have a transaction in progress.
    
    Deprecated since version 1.4: this parameter is deprecated and will be removed in SQLAlchemy 2.0
    
*   **execution\_options**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.execution_options)
     –
    
    a dictionary of execution options that will be passed to [`Connection.execution_options()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/connections.html#sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.execution_options "sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.execution_options")
    , **when the connection is first procured only**. If the connection is already present within the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
    , a warning is emitted and the arguments are ignored.
    
    See also
    
    [Setting Transaction Isolation Levels / DBAPI AUTOCOMMIT](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_transaction.html#session-transaction-isolation)
    
*   **\*\*kw**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.connection.params.**kw)
     – deprecated; use bind\_arguments
    

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
delete(_instance_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.delete "Link to this definition")

Mark an instance as deleted.

The object is assumed to be either [persistent](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-persistent)
 or [detached](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-detached)
 when passed; after the method is called, the object will remain in the [persistent](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-persistent)
 state until the next flush proceeds. During this time, the object will also be a member of the [`Session.deleted`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.deleted "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.deleted")
 collection.

When the next flush proceeds, the object will move to the [deleted](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-deleted)
 state, indicating a `DELETE` statement was emitted for its row within the current transaction. When the transaction is successfully committed, the deleted object is moved to the [detached](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/glossary.html#term-detached)
 state and is no longer present within this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
.

See also

[Deleting](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#session-deleting)
 - at [Basics of Using a Session](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_basics.html#id1)

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
deleted[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.deleted "Link to this definition")

The set of all instances marked as ‘deleted’ within this `Session`

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
dirty[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.dirty "Link to this definition")

The set of all persistent instances considered dirty.

E.g.:

some\_mapped\_object in session.dirty

Instances are considered dirty when they were modified but not deleted.

Note that this ‘dirty’ calculation is ‘optimistic’; most attribute-setting or collection modification operations will mark an instance as ‘dirty’ and place it in this set, even if there is no net change to the attribute’s value. At flush time, the value of each attribute is compared to its previously saved value, and if there’s no net change, no SQL operation will occur (this is a more expensive operation so it’s only done at flush time).

To check if an instance has actionable net changes to its attributes, use the [`Session.is_modified()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.is_modified "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.is_modified")
 method.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
enable\_relationship\_loading(_obj_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "Link to this definition")

Associate an object with this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 for related object loading.

Warning

[`enable_relationship_loading()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading")
 exists to serve special use cases and is not recommended for general use.

Accesses of attributes mapped with [`relationship()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/relationship_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship "sqlalchemy.orm.relationship")
 will attempt to load a value from the database using this [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 as the source of connectivity. The values will be loaded based on foreign key and primary key values present on this object - if not present, then those relationships will be unavailable.

The object will be attached to this session, but will **not** participate in any persistence operations; its state for almost all purposes will remain either “transient” or “detached”, except for the case of relationship loading.

Also note that backrefs will often not work as expected. Altering a relationship-bound attribute on the target object may not fire off a backref event, if the effective value is what was already loaded from a foreign-key-holding value.

The [`Session.enable_relationship_loading()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading")
 method is similar to the `load_on_pending` flag on [`relationship()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/relationship_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship "sqlalchemy.orm.relationship")
. Unlike that flag, [`Session.enable_relationship_loading()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading")
 allows an object to remain transient while still being able to load related items.

To make a transient object associated with a [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 via [`Session.enable_relationship_loading()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading")
 pending, add it to the [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 using [`Session.add()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.add")
 normally. If the object instead represents an existing identity in the database, it should be merged using [`Session.merge()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.merge "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.merge")
.

[`Session.enable_relationship_loading()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading "sqlalchemy.orm.Session.enable_relationship_loading")
 does not improve behavior when the ORM is used normally - object references should be constructed at the object level, not at the foreign key level, so that they are present in an ordinary way before flush() proceeds. This method is not intended for general use.

See also

[`relationship.load_on_pending`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/relationship_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.load_on_pending "sqlalchemy.orm.relationship")
 - this flag allows per-relationship loading of many-to-ones on items that are pending.

[`make_transient_to_detached()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.make_transient_to_detached "sqlalchemy.orm.make_transient_to_detached")
 - allows for an object to be added to a [`Session`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session "sqlalchemy.orm.Session")
 without SQL emitted, which then will unexpire attributes on access.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.orm.Session.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/orm/session_api.html#sqlalchemy.orm.Session)
