Visitor and Traversal Utilities[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#visitor-and-traversal-utilities "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================================================

The [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#module-sqlalchemy.sql.visitors "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors")
 module consists of classes and functions that serve the purpose of generically **traversing** a Core SQL expression structure. This is not unlike the Python `ast` module in that is presents a system by which a program can operate upon each component of a SQL expression. Common purposes this serves are locating various kinds of elements such as [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
 or [`BindParameter`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.BindParameter "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.BindParameter")
 objects, as well as altering the state of the structure such as replacing certain FROM clauses with others.

Note

the [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#module-sqlalchemy.sql.visitors "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors")
 module is an internal API and is not fully public. It is subject to change and may additionally not function as expected for use patterns that aren’t considered within SQLAlchemy’s own internals.

The [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#module-sqlalchemy.sql.visitors "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors")
 module is part of the **internals** of SQLAlchemy and it is not usually used by calling application code. It is however used in certain edge cases such as when constructing caching routines as well as when building out custom SQL expressions using the [Custom SQL Constructs and Compilation Extension](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/compiler.html)
.

Visitor/traversal interface and library functions.

SQLAlchemy schema and expression constructs rely on a Python-centric version of the classic “visitor” pattern as the primary way in which they apply functionality. The most common use of this pattern is statement compilation, where individual expression classes match up to rendering methods that produce a string result. Beyond this, the visitor system is also used to inspect expressions for various information and patterns, as well as for the purposes of applying transformations to expressions.

Examples of how the visit system is used can be seen in the source code of for example the `sqlalchemy.sql.util` and the `sqlalchemy.sql.compiler` modules. Some background on clause adaption is also at [https://techspot.zzzeek.org/2008/01/23/expression-transformations/](https://techspot.zzzeek.org/2008/01/23/expression-transformations/)
 .

| Object Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [cloned\_traverse](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse)<br>(obj, opts, visitors) | Clone the given expression structure, allowing modifications by visitors. |
| [ExternalTraversal](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal) | Base class for visitor objects which can traverse externally using the [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")<br> function. |
| [InternalTraversal](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal) | Defines visitor symbols used for internal traversal. |
| [iterate](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate)<br>(obj\[, opts\]) | Traverse the given expression structure, returning an iterator. |
| [replacement\_traverse](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse)<br>(obj, opts, replace) | Clone the given expression structure, allowing element replacement by a given replacement function. |
| [traverse](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse)<br>(obj, opts, visitors) | Traverse and visit the given expression structure using the default iterator. |
| [traverse\_using](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using)<br>(iterator, obj, visitors) | Visit the given expression structure using the given iterator of objects. |
| [Traversible](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible) | Base class for visitable objects, applies the [`TraversibleType`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType")<br> metaclass. |
| [TraversibleType](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType) | Metaclass which assigns dispatch attributes to various kinds of “visitable” classes. |

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal "Link to this definition")

Base class for visitor objects which can traverse externally using the [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")
 function.

**Members**

[chain()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.chain)
, [iterate()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.iterate)
, [traverse()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.traverse)
, [visitor\_iterator](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.visitor_iterator)

Direct usage of the [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")
 function is usually preferred.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal)
chain(_visitor_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.chain "Link to this definition")

‘Chain’ an additional ClauseVisitor onto this ClauseVisitor.

The chained visitor will receive all visit events after this one.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal)
iterate(_obj_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.iterate "Link to this definition")

Traverse the given expression structure, returning an iterator of all elements.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal)
traverse(_obj_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.traverse "Link to this definition")

Traverse and visit the given expression structure.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal)
visitor\_iterator[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.ExternalTraversal.visitor_iterator "Link to this definition")

Iterate through this visitor and each ‘chained’ visitor.

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "Link to this definition")

Defines visitor symbols used for internal traversal.

The [`InternalTraversal`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal")
 class is used in two ways. One is that it can serve as the superclass for an object that implements the various visit methods of the class. The other is that the symbols themselves of [`InternalTraversal`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal")
 are used within the `_traverse_internals` collection. Such as, the [`Case`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case")
 object defines `_traverse_internals` as

\_traverse\_internals \= \[\
    ("value", InternalTraversal.dp\_clauseelement),\
    ("whens", InternalTraversal.dp\_clauseelement\_tuples),\
    ("else\_", InternalTraversal.dp\_clauseelement),\
\]

Above, the [`Case`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case")
 class indicates its internal state as the attributes named `value`, `whens`, and `else_`. They each link to an [`InternalTraversal`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal")
 method which indicates the type of datastructure referred towards.

Using the `_traverse_internals` structure, objects of type `InternalTraversible` will have the following methods automatically implemented:

*   [`Traversible.get_children()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible.get_children "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible.get_children")
    
*   `Traversible._copy_internals()`
    
*   `Traversible._gen_cache_key()`
    

Subclasses can also implement these methods directly, particularly for the `Traversible._copy_internals()` method, when special steps are needed.

**Members**

[dispatch()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dispatch)

Added in version 1.4.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dispatch(_visit\_symbol_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dispatch "Link to this definition")

Given a method from [`InternalTraversal`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal")
, return the corresponding method on a subclass.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_annotations\_key _\= symbol('AK')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_annotations_key "Link to this definition")

Visit the \_annotations\_cache\_key element.

This is a dictionary of additional information about a ClauseElement that modifies its role. It should be included when comparing or caching objects, however generating this key is relatively expensive. Visitors should check the “\_annotations” dict for non-None first before creating this key.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_anon\_name _\= symbol('AN')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_anon_name "Link to this definition")

Visit a potentially “anonymized” string value.

The string value is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_boolean _\= symbol('B')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_boolean "Link to this definition")

Visit a boolean value.

The boolean value is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_clauseelement _\= symbol('CE')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_clauseelement "Link to this definition")

Visit a [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_clauseelement\_list _\= symbol('CL')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_clauseelement_list "Link to this definition")

Visit a list of [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_clauseelement\_tuple _\= symbol('CT')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_clauseelement_tuple "Link to this definition")

Visit a tuple of [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_clauseelement\_tuples _\= symbol('CTS')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_clauseelement_tuples "Link to this definition")

Visit a list of tuples which contain [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_dialect\_options _\= symbol('DO')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_dialect_options "Link to this definition")

Visit a dialect options structure.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_dml\_multi\_values _\= symbol('DML\_MV')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_dml_multi_values "Link to this definition")

Visit the values() multi-valued list of dictionaries of an [`Insert`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Insert "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Insert")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_dml\_ordered\_values _\= symbol('DML\_OV')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_dml_ordered_values "Link to this definition")

Visit the values() ordered tuple list of an [`Update`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Update "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Update")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_dml\_values _\= symbol('DML\_V')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_dml_values "Link to this definition")

Visit the values() dictionary of a [`ValuesBase`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/dml.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ValuesBase "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ValuesBase")
 (e.g. Insert or Update) object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_fromclause\_canonical\_column\_collection _\= symbol('FC')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_fromclause_canonical_column_collection "Link to this definition")

Visit a [`FromClause`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.FromClause "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.FromClause")
 object in the context of the `columns` attribute.

The column collection is “canonical”, meaning it is the originally defined location of the [`ColumnClause`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ColumnClause "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ColumnClause")
 objects. Right now this means that the object being visited is a [`TableClause`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.TableClause "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.TableClause")
 or [`Table`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/metadata.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Table "sqlalchemy.schema.Table")
 object only.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_fromclause\_ordered\_set _\= symbol('CO')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_fromclause_ordered_set "Link to this definition")

Visit an ordered set of [`FromClause`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.FromClause "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.FromClause")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_has\_cache\_key _\= symbol('HC')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_has_cache_key "Link to this definition")

Visit a [`HasCacheKey`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey "sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_has\_cache\_key\_list _\= symbol('HL')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_has_cache_key_list "Link to this definition")

Visit a list of [`HasCacheKey`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey "sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_named\_ddl\_element _\= symbol('DD')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_named_ddl_element "Link to this definition")

Visit a simple named DDL element.

The current object used by this method is the [`Sequence`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/defaults.html#sqlalchemy.schema.Sequence "sqlalchemy.schema.Sequence")
.

The object is only considered to be important for cache key generation as far as its name, but not any other aspects of it.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_operator _\= symbol('O')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_operator "Link to this definition")

Visit an operator.

The operator is a function from the `sqlalchemy.sql.operators` module.

The operator value is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_plain\_dict _\= symbol('PD')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_plain_dict "Link to this definition")

Visit a dictionary with string keys.

The keys of the dictionary should be strings, the values should be immutable and hashable. The dictionary is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_plain\_obj _\= symbol('PO')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_plain_obj "Link to this definition")

Visit a plain python object.

The value should be immutable and hashable, such as an integer. The value is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_prefix\_sequence _\= symbol('PS')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_prefix_sequence "Link to this definition")

Visit the sequence represented by [`HasPrefixes`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.HasPrefixes "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.HasPrefixes")
 or [`HasSuffixes`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.HasSuffixes "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.HasSuffixes")
.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_propagate\_attrs _\= symbol('PA')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_propagate_attrs "Link to this definition")

Visit the propagate attrs dict. This hardcodes to the particular elements we care about right now.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_statement\_hint\_list _\= symbol('SH')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_statement_hint_list "Link to this definition")

Visit the `_statement_hints` collection of a [`Select`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Select "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Select")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_string _\= symbol('S')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_string "Link to this definition")

Visit a plain string value.

Examples include table and column names, bound parameter keys, special keywords such as “UNION”, “UNION ALL”.

The string value is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_string\_clauseelement\_dict _\= symbol('CD')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_string_clauseelement_dict "Link to this definition")

Visit a dictionary of string keys to [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_string\_list _\= symbol('SL')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_string_list "Link to this definition")

Visit a list of strings.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_string\_multi\_dict _\= symbol('MD')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_string_multi_dict "Link to this definition")

Visit a dictionary of string keys to values which may either be plain immutable/hashable or [`HasCacheKey`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey "sqlalchemy.sql.traversals.HasCacheKey")
 objects.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_table\_hint\_list _\= symbol('TH')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_table_hint_list "Link to this definition")

Visit the `_hints` collection of a [`Select`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Select "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Select")
 object.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_type _\= symbol('T')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_type "Link to this definition")

Visit a [`TypeEngine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/type_api.html#sqlalchemy.types.TypeEngine "sqlalchemy.types.TypeEngine")
 object

The type object is considered to be significant for cache key generation.

_attribute_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal)
dp\_unknown\_structure _\= symbol('UK')_[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal.dp_unknown_structure "Link to this definition")

Visit an unknown structure.

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible "Link to this definition")

**Members**

[get\_children()](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible.get_children)

Base class for visitable objects, applies the [`TraversibleType`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType")
 metaclass.

_method_ [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible.`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible)
get\_children(_omit\_attrs\=()_, _\*\*kw_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible.get_children "Link to this definition")

Return immediate child [`Traversible`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible")
 elements of this [`Traversible`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.Traversible")
.

This is used for visit traversal.

\*\*kw may contain flags that change the collection that is returned, for example to return a subset of items in order to cut down on larger traversals, or to return child items from a different context (such as schema-level collections instead of clause-level).

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType(_clsname_, _bases_, _clsdict_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType "Link to this definition")

Metaclass which assigns dispatch attributes to various kinds of “visitable” classes.

Attributes include:

*   The `_compiler_dispatch` method, corresponding to `__visit_name__`. This is called “external traversal” because the caller of each visit() method is responsible for sub-traversing the inner elements of each object. This is appropriate for string compilers and other traversals that need to call upon the inner elements in a specific pattern.
    
*   internal traversal collections `_children_traversal`, `_cache_key_traversal`, `_copy_internals_traversal`, generated from an optional `_traverse_internals` collection of symbols which comes from the [`InternalTraversal`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.InternalTraversal")
     list of symbols. This is called “internal traversal” MARKMARK
    

**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.TraversibleType")
 (`builtins.type`)

_function_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned\_traverse(_obj_, _opts_, _visitors_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse "Link to this definition")

Clone the given expression structure, allowing modifications by visitors.

Traversal usage is the same as that of [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")
. The visitor functions present in the `visitors` dictionary may also modify the internals of the given structure as the traversal proceeds.

The central API feature used by the [`cloned_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse")
 and [`replacement_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse")
 functions, in addition to the [`ClauseElement.get_children()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement.get_children "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement.get_children")
 function that is used to achieve the iteration, is the `ClauseElement._copy_internals()` method. For a [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 structure to support cloning and replacement traversals correctly, it needs to be able to pass a cloning function into its internal members in order to make copies of them.

See also

[`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")

[`replacement_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse")

_function_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate(_obj_, _opts\={}_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate "Link to this definition")

Traverse the given expression structure, returning an iterator.

Traversal is configured to be breadth-first.

The central API feature used by the [`iterate()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate")
 function is the [`ClauseElement.get_children()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement.get_children "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement.get_children")
 method of [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects. This method should return all the [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 objects which are associated with a particular [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
 object. For example, a [`Case`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Case")
 structure will refer to a series of [`ColumnElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ColumnElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ColumnElement")
 objects within its “whens” and “else\_” member variables.

Parameters:

*   **obj**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate.params.obj)
     – [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
     structure to be traversed
    
*   **opts**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate.params.opts)
     – dictionary of iteration options. This dictionary is usually empty in modern usage.
    

_function_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement\_traverse(_obj_, _opts_, _replace_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse "Link to this definition")

Clone the given expression structure, allowing element replacement by a given replacement function.

This function is very similar to the [`cloned_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse")
 function, except instead of being passed a dictionary of visitors, all elements are unconditionally passed into the given replace function. The replace function then has the option to return an entirely new object which will replace the one given. If it returns `None`, then the object is kept in place.

The difference in usage between [`cloned_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.cloned_traverse")
 and [`replacement_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse")
 is that in the former case, an already-cloned object is passed to the visitor function, and the visitor function can then manipulate the internal state of the object. In the case of the latter, the visitor function should only return an entirely different object, or do nothing.

The use case for [`replacement_traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.replacement_traverse")
 is that of replacing a FROM clause inside of a SQL structure with a different one, as is a common use case within the ORM.

_function_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse(_obj_, _opts_, _visitors_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "Link to this definition")

Traverse and visit the given expression structure using the default iterator.

> e.g.:
> 
> from sqlalchemy.sql import visitors
> 
> stmt \= select(some\_table).where(some\_table.c.foo \== 'bar')
> 
> def visit\_bindparam(bind\_param):
>     print("found bound value: %s" % bind\_param.value)
> 
> visitors.traverse(stmt, {}, {"bindparam": visit\_bindparam})

The iteration of objects uses the [`iterate()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate")
 function, which does a breadth-first traversal using a stack.

Parameters:

*   **obj**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse.params.obj)
     – [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
     structure to be traversed
    
*   **opts**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse.params.opts)
     – dictionary of iteration options. This dictionary is usually empty in modern usage.
    
*   **visitors**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse.params.visitors)
     – dictionary of visit functions. The dictionary should have strings as keys, each of which would correspond to the `__visit_name__` of a particular kind of SQL expression object, and callable functions as values, each of which represents a visitor function for that kind of object.
    

_function_ sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse\_using(_iterator_, _obj_, _visitors_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using "Link to this definition")

Visit the given expression structure using the given iterator of objects.

[`traverse_using()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using")
 is usually called internally as the result of the [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")
 function.

Parameters:

*   **iterator**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using.params.iterator)
     – an iterable or sequence which will yield [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
     structures; the iterator is assumed to be the product of the [`iterate()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate")
     function.
    
*   **obj**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using.params.obj)
     – the [`ClauseElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/foundation.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.ClauseElement")
     that was used as the target of the [`iterate()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.iterate")
     function.
    
*   **visitors**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse_using.params.visitors)
     – dictionary of visit functions. See [`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")
     for details on this dictionary.
    

See also

[`traverse()`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/visitors.html#sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse "sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse")

SQL and Generic Functions[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sql-and-generic-functions "Link to this heading")

=====================================================================================================================================

SQL functions are invoked by using the [`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 namespace. See the tutorial at [Working with SQL Functions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/data_select.html#tutorial-functions)
 for background on how to use the [`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 object to render SQL functions in statements.

See also

[Working with SQL Functions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/data_select.html#tutorial-functions)
 - in the [SQLAlchemy 1.4 / 2.0 Tutorial](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/index.html#unified-tutorial)

Function API[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#function-api "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The base API for SQL functions, which provides for the [`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 namespace as well as classes that may be used for extensibility.

| Object Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [AnsiFunction](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction) | Define a function in “ansi” format, which doesn’t render parenthesis. |
| [Function](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function) | Describe a named SQL function. |
| [FunctionElement](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement) | Base for SQL function-oriented constructs. |
| [GenericFunction](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction) | Define a ‘generic’ function. |
| [register\_function](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.register_function)<br>(identifier, fn\[, package\]) | Associate a callable with a particular func. name. |

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction(_\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction "Link to this definition")

Define a function in “ansi” format, which doesn’t render parenthesis.

**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.AnsiFunction")
 ([`sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction")
)

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function(_name_, _\*clauses_, _\*\*kw_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "Link to this definition")

Describe a named SQL function.

The [`Function`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function")
 object is typically generated from the [`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 generation object.

Parameters:

*   **\*clauses**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function.params.*clauses)
     – list of column expressions that form the arguments of the SQL function call.
    
*   **type\_**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function.params.type_)
     – optional [`TypeEngine`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/type_api.html#sqlalchemy.types.TypeEngine "sqlalchemy.types.TypeEngine")
     datatype object that will be used as the return value of the column expression generated by this function call.
    
*   **packagenames**[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function.params.packagenames)
     –
    
    a string which indicates package prefix names to be prepended to the function name when the SQL is generated. The [`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
     generator creates these when it is called using dotted format, e.g.:
    
    func.mypackage.some\_function(col1, col2)
    

See also

[Working with SQL Functions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/data_select.html#tutorial-functions)
 - in the [SQLAlchemy 1.4 / 2.0 Tutorial](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/index.html#unified-tutorial)

[`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 - namespace which produces registered or ad-hoc [`Function`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function")
 instances.

[`GenericFunction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction")
 - allows creation of registered function types.

**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function")
 ([`sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement")
)

_class_ sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement(_\*clauses_, _\*\*kwargs_)[¶](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement "Link to this definition")

Base for SQL function-oriented constructs.

See also

[Working with SQL Functions](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/data_select.html#tutorial-functions)
 - in the [SQLAlchemy 1.4 / 2.0 Tutorial](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/tutorial/index.html#unified-tutorial)

[`Function`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function")
 - named SQL function.

[`func`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.func")
 - namespace which produces registered or ad-hoc [`Function`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.Function")
 instances.

[`GenericFunction`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.GenericFunction")
 - allows creation of registered function types.

**Class signature**

class [`sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/functions.html#sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement "sqlalchemy.sql.functions.FunctionElement")
 ([`sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Executable`](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/selectable.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Executable "sqlalchemy.sql.expression.Executable")
